compiled_state.title: As a developer, the adversarial review catches consumer impact gaps and persists findings for post-mortems
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Extract red team and blue team into dedicated plugin agents (dso:red-team-reviewer, dso:blue-team-filter). Add 6th taxonomy category (Consumer Impact / Operational Readiness) and Consumer Enumeration directive to red team. Add artifact persistence to blue team.
**Why**: Dedicated agents allow stronger guidance and better prompt management. Consumer impact taxonomy catches downstream breakage. Persisted findings enable post-mortem analysis.
**Scope**:
- IN: Two new agent definition files (plugins/dso/agents/), modify preplanning to dispatch dedicated agents instead of generic, 6th taxonomy category, Consumer Enumeration directive, artifact persistence (ARTIFACTS_DIR), one-line ticket comment reference
- OUT: Feasibility review (S2), completion verification (S5), integration research (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, dso:red-team-reviewer includes Consumer Impact / Operational Readiness as 6th taxonomy category ← Satisfies SC3
- When this story is complete, dso:red-team-reviewer includes Consumer Enumeration directive that identifies known consumers before analyzing stories ← Satisfies SC3
- When this story is complete, dso:blue-team-filter persists full exchange (accepted + rejected with rationale) to ARTIFACTS_DIR/adversarial-review-<epic-id>.json where ARTIFACTS_DIR is resolved via get_artifacts_dir ← Satisfies SC3
- When this story is complete, one-line ticket comment references the artifact file ← Satisfies SC3
- When this story is complete, preplanning dispatches dedicated agents instead of generic agents ← Satisfies SC3
- When this story is complete, existing red/blue team behavior is preserved for the original 5 taxonomy categories ← regression safety
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must preserve existing red/blue team behavior before adding new taxonomy — regression risk. Extract first, then add new features.

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks completed and committed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
